rationale: One real skill-vs-workflow drift. The Runner-dispatch tuple `{slug, spec, target, bead_id?, round, lenses[], mix}` omits `claude_sub_on_quota`. The shipped ms-panel.js cannot read config (its header: reading config is intentionally outside ALLOWED_CLI — 'the invoking skill resolves the flag and passes it in') and fail-closes absent args to false (ms-panel.js:455 `=== true`). Config default is TRUE (config_test.go:375). So every skill-composed workflow invocation silently disables the substitution ladder: a quota-walled codex slot goes MISSING and the gate Blocks a panel the config said should substitute — the exact event (codex walls) the ladder exists for. Spec R4 says substitution is driven by the config flag ('falsified... if it ignores the 109 claude_sub_on_quota flag') and spec.md:40 assigns the flag-read to the runner-dispatch via `mindspec config show` (which renders `claude_sub_on_quota:` — config.go:196). The implementer copied R6's tuple literally without reconciling against the shipped Bead-2 contract. Everything else holds: mirrors byte-identical, all judgment/skills-path sections retained verbatim, Step-0 double-registration explicitly prevented ('do not separately walk Step 0 through the Anti-patterns section'), runner values/default/degradation match the config validator and ADR-0040. One-line fixes.
concrete_changes_required: ms-panel-run/SKILL.md (both mirrors), Runner-dispatch claude-code-workflow bullet: add `claude_sub_on_quota` to the invocation tuple, resolved from `panel.substitution.claude_sub_on_quota` in the same `mindspec config show` read (it is rendered there) — e.g. `{slug, spec, target, bead_id?, round, lenses[], mix, claude_sub_on_quota}` with a clause noting the workflow cannot read config and fail-closes to no-substitution when the flag is not passed. Update the runbook.md tuple mention to match.

ms-panel-tally/SKILL.md (both mirrors), Workflow-path note: in the narrowed-job sentence, name the Step-1 Allow-screen alongside consolidation — e.g. 'narrows to Step 1's artifact-gate Allow-screen (still mandatory before any merge handoff), Step 2 consolidation, and the merge terminal'. Currently the one sentence describing the workflow-path job omits the one HARD-block judgment act on an Allow (the fbel.5-class screen), inviting a consume-result-then-merge reading on the path where no operator habits exist yet.
findings: DRIFT (load-bearing): skill dispatch tuple omits claude_sub_on_quota; ms-panel.js:455 fail-closes absent arg to false while config default is true (internal/config/config_test.go:375) and the workflow's own args doc says the invoking skill must resolve and pass it. Net effect: workflow-path panels never substitute on a codex quota wall regardless of config — spec R4's config-driven substitution is unsatisfiable via this skill. Root cause is upstream (spec/plan R6 tuple predates the Bead-2 contract), but this bead owns the seam and spec.md:40 explicitly assigns the substitution-flag config-read to the runner-dispatch.

WEAKENING (moderate, prose): tally Workflow-path note's 'narrows to Step 2 consolidation and the merge terminal' omits the Step-1 Allow-screen from the narrowed job list. The screen text itself survives verbatim and the note says '§ Artifact gates ... unchanged on both paths', so the invariant is retained-but-obscured rather than lost; still worth one clause given the $417 fbel.5 postmortem is exactly this screen being skipped on an Allow. (Spec R7's own phrasing has the same shape; the fix does not contradict the spec.)

VERIFIED-OK: Step-0 double-registration prevented — the workflow bullet says 'do not separately walk Step 0 through the Anti-patterns section below on this path', naming Step 0 explicitly; dispatch is the first section and opens with 'before doing anything else'. No label on the Step-0 heading itself is acceptable.

VERIFIED-OK: runner values + default match internal/config validator (claude-code-skills default, claude-code-workflow, external — config.go:509); `grep '^runner:'` matches config show's render (config.go:254); host-degradation rule is spec-backed (spec.md:72, ADR-0040 capability tiers).

VERIFIED-OK: retention — diff is +70/−0; Launch the panel / Codex failure detection / Working directory matters / Anti-patterns gated not deleted; Slot lens defaults unlabelled (the workflow path's sole authoring step); tally's Consolidate, Artifact gates (incl. Allow-screen text), After a halt, Escape hatch, Abandon all intact; both mirror pairs byte-identical.

NOTE (no change required): `mix` shape ([{family,count}]) is validated workflow-side; the skill's 'resolved panel: reviewer mix' pointer is sufficient. `sha` correctly omitted from the skill tuple (advisory-only per Bead-2).
